steps.title: Identify the independent clauses at the boundary
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses requiring proper separation. The first is: “The pedagogy of the Suzuki method is rooted in several central [noun].” The second is: “The method emphasizes playing instruments from a very young age and teaches students as young as three to play simple classical pieces.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for sentence boundaries
steps.content: Two independent clauses cannot be separated by a comma alone; they require a period or semicolon. Choice A correctly uses a period after “tenets,” ending the first clause and allowing the second sentence to begin with “Developed by Japanese violinist Shinichi Suzuki, who sought to parallel the linguistic learning environment,” — a participial phrase that modifies “the method.” Choices B and D create comma splices by leaving only a comma between the relative clause and the following independent clause; choice C produces ungrammatical syntax. That matches choice A — A is correct.
